subject: Add reusable gardening JSONL contract and central event stream
rationale: Centralize deterministic gardening decision records so tooling can be reused across sessions.

subject: Initialize git-history gardening session and deterministic logs
rationale: Bootstrap git-history pruning workflow with explicit review gate and machine-processable log trail.

subject: Propose fixup of 2de2e4b into 0486637
rationale: Candidate identified during analysis-only pass; waiting for explicit approval before any history edits.

subject: Propose fixup of 1268d21 into 3bf3b15
rationale: Candidate identified during analysis-only pass; waiting for explicit approval before any history edits.

subject: Execute approved fixup proposal GHP-001
rationale: Executed approved history cleanup to fold commit 2de2e4b into its parent gardening feature commit.

subject: Execute approved fixup proposal GHP-002
rationale: Executed approved history cleanup to fold commit 1268d21 into its parent partner feature commit.

subject: Analysis pass found no new history cleanup proposals
rationale: Current commit window is coherent; no proposal-worthy follow-up commits identified.

subject: Analysis pass found no new history cleanup proposals after signoff archival updates
rationale: Current commit window remains coherent; no proposal-worthy follow-up commits identified.

subject: Add Samwise done-for-day protocol and diary logging contract
rationale: Partner continuity and self-improvement needed a deterministic daily closeout ritual and structured record.

subject: Enforce explicit AJ approval per git commit in Samwise workflows
rationale: AJ requested strict per-commit authorization so Samwise never creates a commit without explicit consent for that specific commit.

subject: Constrain standing commit permission to AJ-approved non-main dedicated worktrees
rationale: AJ clarified that Persona/Pack/Session commit authority is only valid when operating in a non-main dedicated worktree with explicit auto-commit approval for that worktree.

subject: Add reusable Gardening v2 checklist and require it for maintenance passes
rationale: A deterministic checklist improves consistency for drift scan quality, proposal discipline, policy synchronization, validation evidence, and closeout readiness.

subject: Explicitly define self-gardening approval parity in gardener files
rationale: Clarifying self-gardening behavior prevents policy ambiguity and ensures gardener-owned artifact updates use the same analysis-first and human-gated review flow.

subject: Name the pack gardener persona as Rosie while preserving stable persona ID
rationale: A named gardener persona improves readability and collaboration while retaining compatibility through unchanged persona ID references.

subject: Codify Rosie dedicated lane upkeep workflow with explicit lane/main sync guardrails
rationale: A dedicated operating surface is required so routine Rosie gardening runs stay repeatable, review-gated, and traceable from lane commits through mainline integration.

subject: Align Rosie upkeep scope boundaries across kit, intent, directive, and persona
rationale: Self-gardening audit found lane policy bleed and ambiguous integration boundaries; tightening scope and branch requirements keeps Rosie runs deterministic and approval-gated.

subject: Harden Rosie directive/persona wording for explicit commit mode and lane scope guardrails
rationale: Focused Rosie audit found low-severity ambiguity where commit authorization and wrong-lane constraints were inherited but not explicit in local wording.

subject: Adopt recurring closeout checklist for pack/session maintenance
rationale: A single closeout checklist prevents maintenance and logging steps from being skipped during milestone or phase transitions.

subject: Record git-history analysis pass #4 with no pending proposals
rationale: No narrow follow-up commits were found in the current review window, so keep-as-is remains the deterministic recommendation.

subject: Clarify Samwise closeout as checkpoint-based rather than literal calendar-day based
rationale: AJ clarified that closeout timing is pause/reflection based, so governance wording now avoids literal workday boundaries while preserving continuity and commit guardrails.

subject: Add Samwise coffee-checkpoint startup session for friendly neutral orientation
rationale: AJ requested a non-literal, neutral startup mode with explicit track choices before deep execution work begins.

subject: Harden coffee-checkpoint flow with explicit fallback and aligned review gating
rationale: Critical review identified a small risk mismatch and missing fallback semantics for live external update tracks; updates keep startup behavior safer and more deterministic.

subject: Plan gardening toolchain and implement phase-1 observability streams
rationale: Health snapshots and recommendation outcome telemetry are required before recommendation-scoring improvements can be measured and tuned.

subject: Implement deterministic `GREC-*` recommendation scoring stream and validator enforcement
rationale: Ranking recommendations with deterministic score math and explanation fields is required to prioritize phase-3/4 upkeep work consistently.

subject: Implement coverage auditing and policy/safety preflight detectors for Rosie upkeep
rationale: Closing phase-3/4 gaps with deterministic detectors prevents silent policy drift and catches unresolved pack graph references before non-trivial edits execute.

subject: Standardize Samwise role language as Trusted Partner with AJ Trusted Partner as the canonical agent-facing label
rationale: AJ wants one role title that fits both human collaboration and agent handoffs, so the partner stack now makes the Trusted Partner framing explicit without changing stable PersonaKit IDs.

validationNotes: `./Scripts/check-gardening-logs.sh` failed because existing pack-coverage snapshots are stale relative to current pack counts

subject: Add reusable Orbit meeting transcript protocol and core kit above any single meeting participant
rationale: Meeting-note structure drift showed that transcript rules need to live in shared pack artifacts instead of relying on Samwise memory or ad hoc correction.

keyRules: do not edit another speaker's turn content without explicit AJ instruction
append new turns instead of normalizing prior ones when possible
validationNotes: `./Scripts/check-gardening-logs.sh` failed because pack-coverage snapshots are stale relative to current pack counts

subject: Add a generic Samwise workflow for staffing a squad, checking role coverage, and drafting the first plan before execution
rationale: Samwise already had partner sync, reverse-interview, and squad oversight workflows, but not one reusable front door for forming a team and identifying missing roles before execution begins.

validationNotes: `./Scripts/check-gardening-logs.sh` failed because existing pack-coverage snapshots are stale relative to current pack counts

subject: Add durable planning logs, a role-gap remediation loop, and a reusable Samwise orchestration kit
rationale: Samwise needs durable planning evidence, an explicit missing-role closure loop, and one reusable kit spanning planning through retrospective expectations before AJ can safely step away from brief-to-delivery orchestration.

validationNotes: `./Scripts/check-gardening-logs.sh` failed because existing pack-coverage snapshots are stale relative to current pack counts

subject: Teach coffee checkpoint mode to wake Samwise from the latest continuity artifacts
rationale: AJ wants coffee mode to behave like a reliable new-thread wake-up ritual, so resume-context now needs deterministic diary-first continuity loading instead of relying on prompt phrasing alone.

supportingSources: latest relevant planning review or planning log
validationNotes: `./Scripts/check-gardening-logs.sh` failed because pack-coverage snapshots are stale relative to current pack counts

subject: Refresh stale pack-coverage telemetry after Samwise and Orbit planning pack growth
rationale: Continuity artifacts and validators should report real pack-graph drift rather than stale counts from an older snapshot, especially before Orbit planning continues.

details.resolvedIssue: Latest pack-coverage snapshot now matches current persona, kit, directive, intent, essential, and session counts.

subject: Resolve the remaining Orbit rerun contract gaps around kickoff approval, explicit attempt-isolated evidence paths, and continuity logging
rationale: The Orbit rerun cleanup needed one more bounded pass so the staged hold state, exact `orbit-1` artifact destinations, and Samwise continuity trail all agreed before any new attempt starts.

details.laneApprovalClarification: `codex/orbit-1` remains manifest-approved while kickoff still requires explicit AJ start approval

subject: Add a dedicated Samwise Orbit execution session so startup and live-lane orchestration are separate
rationale: The Orbit rerun flow needed a stronger execution surface so Samwise would route real implementation and review work through explicit sessions instead of letting the startup pass collapse into solo implementation.

details.environmentNote: External isolated worktrees may trigger approval prompts in the desktop environment for bootstrap, build, test, or file-writing commands.

subject: Formalize the Samwise session-review method as a named PersonaKit workflow
rationale: A reusable Samwise review surface makes session audits deterministic, findings-first, and easier to repeat before structural pack/session changes.

subject: Harden the Samwise session-review workflow around MCP-first session resolution, durable review artifacts, and fail-closed MCP-gap handling
rationale: Samwise session reviews should normalize session id-or-path input through PersonaKit MCP, persist durable review artifacts, and stop cleanly when MCP is unavailable instead of freehanding the stack manually.

subject: Slim the Orbit startup session so it owns staging and handoff only, with explicit startup artifact paths
rationale: The Orbit startup surface should require the exact handoff artifacts it depends on and should not keep retrospective-review ownership that belongs to the execution session.

validationNotes: `personakit trace_session samwise-orbit-build-rerun` shows only `orbit-build-rerun-review` as a directive intent
`personakit export --session samwise-orbit-build-rerun` shows the new required startup artifact paths and no `worktree-squad-retrospective-review` reference

subject: Make Samwise leaner by default and push broader hiring/orchestration context into explicit session kit overrides
rationale: Samwise should behave like a stable trusted-partner identity layer by default, while broader hiring and orchestration context should be loaded only by the sessions that actually need it.

validationNotes: `personakit trace_session samwise-orbit-build-rerun` now resolves only `trusted-partner-core` plus `orbit-build-rerun-core`
`personakit trace_session samwise-squad-planning` still resolves explicit hiring and orchestration coverage via session overrides

subject: Split the broad Samwise orchestration boundary into workflow-aligned planning, oversight, and retrospective kits
rationale: Samwise sessions should load only the planning, live-delivery, and retrospective context they actually need instead of inheriting unrelated workflow families by side effect through one shared orchestration override.

subject: Tighten the Samwise orchestration split after red-pen review by removing unnecessary retrospective calibration context from Orbit execution and clarifying the remaining intent-level ballast
rationale: The first split reduced kit-level scope bleed, but Orbit execution still re-inherited retrospective calibration context and the continuity wording overstated how lean the oversight stack now is.

details.remainingScopeNote: `samwise-worktree-squad-oversight` still inherits some non-oversight ballast through `gated-worktree-delivery` intent essentials.
validationNotes: `swift run personakit export --root .personakit --session samwise-orbit-rerun-execution` no longer resolves `samwise-retrospective-core`

subject: Slim the Samwise coffee wake-up surface so it uses diary and brief context by default instead of loading planning contracts
rationale: Coffee mode should stay lightweight and orientation-first, with diary and brief artifacts as the primary wake-up sources rather than planning and planning-log contracts loaded into the startup surface by default.

resumeContextPriority: partner-context continuity notes when the brief is still unclear
details.remainingInheritedContext: `samwise-daily-closeout` still resolves through `trusted-partner-core`, so this pass removed the direct coffee-intent overload rather than every inherited continuity artifact.
validationNotes: `swift run personakit export --root .personakit --session samwise-coffee-checkpoint` no longer resolves planning or planning-log contracts directly

subject: Make Orbit rerun execution require an explicit, distinct specialist session map at the intent-contract layer
rationale: Orbit execution already described a five-route specialist map in directive and essential prose, but the intent still left those routes optional and did not declare a deterministic distinctness rule in machine-readable metadata.

details.toolingVisibility: Existing MCP intent explain/compare outputs now expose `parameterConstraints`; invocation-time rejection of duplicated supplied values still depends on a future generic intent-runner surface.

subject: Stop inheriting closeout protocol through the Samwise base kit and keep it explicit through the dedicated closeout workflow
rationale: Closeout is a real Samwise capability, but it should not broaden coffee, session-review, and other narrow startup/review surfaces through the default trusted-partner kit.

details.explicitCloseoutSurface: `samwise-daily-closeout` remains explicit through `run-samwise-daily-closeout` plus the `samwise-daily-closeout` intent.
validationNotes: `swift run personakit export --root .personakit --session samwise-coffee-checkpoint` no longer resolves `samwise-daily-closeout`
`swift run personakit export --root .personakit --session samwise-daily-closeout` still resolves `samwise-daily-closeout` through its dedicated directive/intent stack

subject: Add a tombstone translation surface so retired artifact IDs stay interpretable without rewriting historical records
rationale: Historical reviews and append-only logs may continue to mention retired artifact IDs or file paths, so the repo needs an additive translation layer that preserves recorded history while mapping legacy references to the current pack graph.

details.appendOnlyRule: Historical markdown and JSONL records remain unchanged; new interpretation should be recorded through tombstones and additive continuity notes.

subject: Keep planning contracts isolated to squad-planning while slimming startup and execution surfaces to explicit handoff context
rationale: The real planning layer was already isolated, but live delivery and Orbit handoff surfaces still carried planning/tracking mass through delivery-intent essentials and directive prose that told operators to load broad planning artifact sets.

details.handoffBoundary: Startup and execution surfaces now consume explicit handoff artifacts and approved checkpoint boundaries instead of generic planning artifact buckets.

subject: Make Conventional Commit format a universal repo constraint instead of a persona-local habit
rationale: Recent commit history showed drift away from Conventional Commit format, so the rule needs to live in one essential that every persona already loads rather than depending on local memory or one workflow family.

details.rule: Use Conventional Commit format for git commits: `type(scope): summary` when a clear scope exists, otherwise `type: summary`.
details.coverageNote: Every current persona resolves `tools-and-constraints` through at least one default kit.

subject: Keep retrospective format, template, and schema contracts on closeout-only surfaces instead of active execution stacks
rationale: The mixed worktree squad log contract and the base execution kit were still pulling Starfish/template/calibration context into live delivery and Orbit execution surfaces before closeout actually began.

subject: Tighten the retrospective boundary split so execution wording is handoff-specific and closeout still carries calibration
rationale: The first split removed most retrospective mass from active execution, but Orbit execution prose still referenced a generic retrospective artifact bucket, the live delivery directive still named one mixed log contract, and the main retrospective closeout intent no longer carried calibration benchmarks.

details.executionHandoffWording: Orbit execution now points to the explicit `retrospectivePath` parameter instead of a generic retrospective artifact bucket.
details.closeoutCalibration: `worktree-squad-retrospective-review` once again includes `worktree-squad-calibration-benchmarks` so the primary closeout path keeps calibration context.
details.contractNaming: The gated delivery directive now names `worktree-squad-loop-log-contract` for active loop entries and `worktree-squad-retrospective-log-contract` for assignment closeout.

subject: Align session-directory lifecycle labels with the intended maturity of validated Samwise specialist lanes
rationale: The session directory and lifecycle legend were treating `candidate` as both “specialized” and “provisional,” which understated validated Samwise specialist lanes that continuity records already treated as approved workflows.

details.definitionChange: `active` now includes validated specialist lanes when they are the approved workflow for trigger-specific conditions, while `candidate` is reserved for provisional or not-yet-promoted lanes.

subject: Keep Orbit startup and playbook contracts reusable by replacing ambient retrospective lookup and dated attempt paths with explicit handoff inputs
rationale: After shared-pack right-sizing, the remaining Orbit-local drift came from startup still loading retrospectives via “latest” wording and from the reusable playbook embedding one dated `orbit-1` packet set that should instead live in attempt-specific rerun artifacts.

details.startupContract: Orbit startup now requires an explicit `retrospectivePath` alongside the existing rerun-prep and execution handoff paths.
details.playbookBoundary: The reusable playbook now specifies stable attempt-artifact categories and requires the active rerun-prep note to carry the exact filenames/paths for the current run.

subject: Rename the active Orbit startup and execution pack family so the pair reads as startup plus execution instead of overloaded build/rerun wording
rationale: The Orbit workflow boundaries were already solid, but the active IDs still made startup and execution harder to parse than they needed to be, especially for humans and agents reading the session directory or pack graph under time pressure.

details.historicalHandling: Append-only historical records remain untouched and now translate through the tombstone index instead of relying on live aliases.
validationNotes: Renamed startup and execution traces/exports resolved cleanly on the active IDs.
